seo.title: Virginia Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in Virginia — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license virginia, dental assistant certification virginia, RDA virginia, DANB CDA virginia, dental assistant radiography virginia

board.name: Virginia Board of Dentistry
board.mailing_address:
Virginia Board of Dentistry
Perimeter Center
9960 Mayland Drive, Suite 300
Henrico, VA 23233-1463

license_types.title: Dental Assistant (no state license required)
license_types.description: No state-level credential required for general dental assistants in Virginia.

steps.title: Meet Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you meet the qualifications for Expanded Duties Dental Assistant (EDDA) registration, which typically includes graduation from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program OR completion of a board-approved expanded duties course AND certification by DANB OR 2 years of full-time dental assisting experience, plus current Basic Life Support (BLS) or CPR certification.

steps.title: Register an Account on DHP Online Services
steps.description: Navigate to the DHP Online Services portal at https://www.license.dhp.virginia.gov/apply/. Click 'Register a Person' to create a new user account with a unique username and password. This account will be used to manage your application and future renewals.

steps.title: Complete and Submit the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your DHP Online Services account. Select 'Apply for a New License/Certification' and choose the 'Expanded Duties Dental Assistant' application. Complete all required sections of the application form and pay the non-refundable application fee online.

steps.title: Arrange for Background Check and Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Submit fingerprints for a criminal background check as instructed by DHP after your application submission. Have official transcripts sent directly from your CODA-accredited program to the Virginia Board of Dentistry. Additionally, provide proof of current CPR/BLS certification, and if applicable, proof of DANB certification.

steps.title: Complete the Virginia Jurisprudence Examination
steps.description: Take and pass the Virginia Dental Jurisprudence Examination. This exam is often available online through the DHP portal or linked resources and assesses your knowledge of Virginia's dental laws and regulations.

steps.title: Receive Your Registration
steps.description: The Virginia Board of Dentistry will review your completed application and all supporting documentation. Once approved, your Expanded Duties Dental Assistant registration will be issued and will be viewable on the DHP License Lookup portal.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Virginia does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

faqs.question: Does Virginia require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: No, Virginia does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Virginia?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Virginia.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Virginia?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Virginia?
faqs.answer: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Virginia. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

reciprocity.notes: Virginia does not license general dental assistants. Radiography certification required for x-rays.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Virginia
hero.dek: To become a registered Expanded Duties Dental Assistant in Virginia, applicants must apply online through the DHP Online Services portal, completing the application and submitting required documentation.

official_links.registration_steps: First-time applicants must register an account by clicking 'Register a Person' on the DHP Online Services portal and following the prompts to create a username and password. Once logged in, navigate to 'Apply for a New License/Certification' to begin the application for Expanded Duties Dental Assistant.
